title: SHD CLI Demo вҖ” Agent-First Coda Interface

pages.title: Why SHD?
pages.content:
# Why SHD?

The Coda MCP server gives agents raw API access. SHD wraps that into an **agent-optimized CLI** that solves real problems we hit building agent workflows.

## The Problem

Agents using the raw MCP server:
- Burn tokens on massive JSON responses (200+ tokens per call)
- Can't read Coda docs with standard file tools (Read, Grep, Glob)
- Must chain 30+ API calls to scaffold a doc
- Get raw HTTP errors with no guidance
- Have no way to clean up text before it hits Coda

## What SHD Adds

| Feature | Raw MCP | SHD CLI |
|---|---|---|
| Token usage | Full JSON responses | `--pick` extracts only what you need (90% reduction) |
| Doc reading | API calls per page/table | `shd sync` materializes docs as files |
| Doc creation | 30+ chained API calls | `shd doc_scaffold` вҖ” one command |
| Text quality | Whatever the agent writes | `--polish` auto-cleans via Claude |
| Tool discovery | Query every time | 24h cache with `--compact` schemas |
| Error handling | Raw HTTP codes | Structured JSON with recovery hints |
| Security | None | Prompt injection detection, input validation |

pages.title: Demo Flow
pages.content:
# Demo Flow

This is the script for the live demo. Each section is a live CLI interaction.

## 1. The Welcome Screen
```
shd
```
Clean, compact. Shows quick-start commands. Compare with `shd --help` for the full reference.

## 2. Discover Tools (no docs needed)
```
shd discover --compact
shd discover table_create --compact
```
Agents can self-serve tool schemas. `--compact` gives 5 lines instead of 300.

## 3. Token Efficiency with --pick
```
shd whoami
shd whoami --pick name
```
First call: ~200 tokens. Second: ~5 tokens. Same info, 95% fewer tokens.

## 4. Scaffold a Complete Doc (one command)
```
shd doc_scaffold --json @blueprint.json --pick docUri,browserLink
```
Creates doc + pages + content + tables + rows. Would be 30+ raw API calls.

## 5. Sync to Filesystem
```
shd sync --doc-url '<url from step 4>'
ls .coda/docs/
cat .coda/docs/*/CONTEXT.md
grep 'Status' .coda/docs/*/pages/tables/*/rows.ndjson
```
Now agents can use standard file tools. No more API calls to read.

## 6. Claude + SHD Together
Open Claude Code and ask it to:
- Read the synced doc from `.coda/`
- Add a new row to a table
- Create a new page with content
- Search for rows matching a condition

Claude uses `shd` as a Bash tool вҖ” no MCP config needed.

## 7. The --polish Flag
```
shd page_create_with_content --json '{...}' --polish
```
Text goes through Claude Haiku for grammar/style cleanup before hitting Coda.

## 8. Auto-Sync After Creation
```
shd doc_scaffold --json @blueprint.json --sync
```
Creates the doc AND syncs it to `.coda/` in one shot. Background process handles the wait.

pages.title: Key Talking Points
pages.content:
# Key Talking Points

## 1. Agents Need Files, Not APIs
The biggest insight: agents are best at reading files. `shd sync` bridges Coda's API world with the agent's file world. After sync, an agent can `grep` across all tables, `cat` any page, and use its native code tools.

## 2. Token Efficiency Is Everything
`--pick` isn't a nice-to-have вҖ” it's the difference between an agent that works and one that hits context limits. A `whoami` call goes from 200 tokens to 5.

## 3. Compound Operations Save Agent Reasoning
`doc_scaffold` doesn't just save API calls вҖ” it saves the agent from having to figure out the right sequence of 30+ calls. The CLI encodes that knowledge.

## 4. No MCP Config Required
Claude Code can use `shd` directly as a Bash tool. No server setup, no JSON-RPC, no config files. Just `shd <tool> --json '{...}'`. The MCP mode exists for tools that need it, but the CLI-first design means zero setup.

## 5. Built for the Agent Loop
Every feature was designed for agents:
- `--pick` вҶ’ minimize tokens
- `--sync` вҶ’ enable file-based reading
- `--fuzzy` вҶ’ recover from typos
- `--polish` вҶ’ fix text quality
- `--sanitize` вҶ’ defend against injection
- `@file` payloads вҶ’ avoid shell escaping
- Structured errors вҶ’ agents can parse and recover

## 6. Dogfooding
This demo doc was created using `shd doc_scaffold`. The CLI builds its own docs.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.content:
# Architecture

## How It Works
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Agent      в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  shd CLI     в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Coda MCP в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  (Claude)   в”Ӯв—Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ              в”Ӯв—Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ Endpoint в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
       в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ .coda/    в”Ӯв—Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
            в”Ӯ (files)   в”Ӯ    shd sync
            в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## Design Decisions

**Dynamic dispatch**: No hardcoded commands per resource. All 28+ tools discovered at runtime. New Coda tools work without a CLI rebuild.

**Rust**: Fast startup (~10ms), single binary, no runtime dependencies. Agents shouldn't wait for the CLI.

**Clap for CLI**: Standard Rust CLI framework. Clean help, shell completions, environment variable support.

**Schema caching**: Tools cached for 24h locally. Client-side validation catches bad payloads before they hit the API.

**SSE parsing**: Coda's MCP endpoint uses Server-Sent Events. The client handles the streaming protocol transparently.
